 17â†’package com.ichi2.anki
 18â†’
 19â†’import android.content.Context
 20â†’import android.content.Intent
 21â†’import android.net.Uri
 22â†’import android.os.Bundle
 23â†’import android.os.Message
 24â†’import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 25â†’import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 26â†’import androidx.core.app.TaskStackBuilder
 27â†’import androidx.core.content.FileProvider
 28â†’import androidx.core.content.IntentCompat
 29â†’import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandler.Companion.storeMessage
 30â†’import com.ichi2.anki.dialogs.DialogHandlerMessage
 31â†’import com.ichi2.anki.preferences.sharedPrefs
 32â†’import com.ichi2.anki.servicelayer.ScopedStorageService
 33â†’import com.ichi2.anki.services.ReminderService
 34â†’import com.ichi2.anki.ui.windows.reviewer.ReviewerFragment
 35â†’import com.ichi2.anki.worker.SyncWorker
 36â†’import com.ichi2.annotations.NeedsTest
 37â†’import com.ichi2.libanki.DeckId
 38â†’import com.ichi2.utils.FileUtil
 39â†’import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.handleFileImport
 40â†’import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.isInvalidViewIntent
 41â†’import com.ichi2.utils.ImportUtils.showImportUnsuccessfulDialog
 42â†’import com.ichi2.utils.IntentUtil.resolveMimeType
 43â†’import com.ichi2.utils.NetworkUtils
 44â†’import com.ichi2.utils.Permissions
 45â†’import com.ichi2.utils.Permissions.hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission
 46â†’import com.ichi2.utils.copyToClipboard
 47â†’import com.ichi2.utils.trimToLength
 48â†’import timber.log.Timber
 49â†’import java.io.File
 50â†’import kotlin.math.max
 51â†’import kotlin.math.min
 52â†’
 53â†’/**
 54â†’ * Class which handles how the application responds to different intents, forcing it to always be single task,
 55â†’ * but allowing custom behavior depending on the intent
 56â†’ * It inherits from [AbstractIntentHandler]
 57â†’ *
 58â†’ * @author Tim
 59â†’ */
 60â†’class IntentHandler : AbstractIntentHandler() {
 61â†’    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 62â†’        // Note: This is our entry point from the launcher with intent: android.intent.action.MAIN
 63â†’        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 64â†’        val intent = intent
 65â†’        Timber.v(intent.toString())
 66â†’        val reloadIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
 67â†’        reloadIntent.setDataAndType(getIntent().data, getIntent().type)
 68â†’        val action = intent.action
 69â†’        // #6157 - We want to block actions that need permissions we don't have, but not the default case
 70â†’        // as this requires nothing
 71â†’        val runIfStoragePermissions = { runnable: () -> Unit -> performActionIfStorageAccessible(reloadIntent, action) { runnable() } }
 72â†’        val launchType = getLaunchType(intent)
 73â†’        // TODO block the UI with some kind of ProgressDialog instead of cancelling the sync work
 74â†’        if (requiresCollectionAccess(launchType)) {
 75â†’            SyncWorker.cancel(this)
 76â†’        }
 77â†’        when (launchType) {
 78â†’            LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT ->
 79â†’                runIfStoragePermissions {
 80â†’                    handleFileImport(fileIntent, reloadIntent, action)
 81â†’                    finish()
 82â†’                }
 83â†’            LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT ->
 84â†’                runIfStoragePermissions {
 85â†’                    onSelectedCsvForImport(fileIntent)
 86â†’                    finish()
 87â†’                }
 88â†’            LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT ->
 89â†’                runIfStoragePermissions {
 90â†’                    handleImageImport(intent)
 91â†’                    finish()
 92â†’                }
 93â†’            LaunchType.SYNC -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleSyncIntent(reloadIntent, action) }
 94â†’            LaunchType.REVIEW -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleReviewIntent(reloadIntent, intent) }
 95â†’            LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW -> {
 96â†’                Timber.d("onCreate() performing default action")
 97â†’                launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
 98â†’            }
 99â†’            LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> {
100â†’                copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent)
101â†’                finish()
102â†’            }
103â†’        }
104â†’    }
105â†’
106â†’    private fun copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent: Intent) {
107â†’        Timber.i("Copying debug info to clipboard")
108â†’        // null string is handled by copyToClipboard in try-catch
109â†’        this.copyToClipboard(
110â†’            text = (intent.getStringExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA)!!),
111â†’            failureMessageId = R.string.about_ankidroid_error_copy_debug_info,
112â†’        )
113â†’    }
114â†’
115â†’    private val fileIntent: Intent
116â†’        get() {
117â†’            return if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
118â†’                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Intent::class.java) ?: intent
119â†’            } else {
120â†’                intent
121â†’            }
122â†’        }
123â†’
124â†’    /**
125â†’     * Execute the runnable if one of the two following conditions are satisfied:
126â†’     *
127â†’     *  * AnkiDroid is using an app-private directory to store user data
128â†’     *  * AnkiDroid is using a legacy directory to store user data but has access to it since storage permission
129â†’     * has been granted (as long as AnkiDroid targeted API < 30, requested legacy storage, and has not been uninstalled since)
130â†’     *
131â†’     */
132â†’    @NeedsTest("clicking a file in 'Files' to import")
133â†’    private fun performActionIfStorageAccessible(
134â†’        reloadIntent: Intent,
135â†’        action: String?,
136â†’        block: () -> Unit,
137â†’    ) {
138â†’        if (grantedStoragePermissions(this, showToast = true)) {
139â†’            Timber.i("User has storage permissions. Running intent: %s", action)
140â†’            block()
141â†’        } else {
142â†’            Timber.i("No Storage Permission, cancelling intent '%s'", action)
143â†’            launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
144â†’        }
145â†’    }
146â†’
147â†’    private fun handleReviewIntent(
148â†’        reloadIntent: Intent,
149â†’        reviewerIntent: Intent,
150â†’    ) {
151â†’        val deckId = intent.getLongExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, 0)
152â†’        Timber.i("Handling intent to review deck '%d'", deckId)
153â†’
154â†’        val reviewIntent =
155â†’            if (sharedPrefs().getBoolean("newReviewer", false)) {
156â†’                ReviewerFragment.getIntent(this)
157â†’            } else {
158â†’                Intent(this, Reviewer::class.java).apply {
159â†’                    addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
160â†’                }
161â†’            }
162â†’        CollectionManager.getColUnsafe().decks.select(deckId)
163â†’        // Clean the stack out under the reviewer to avoid any incorrect activities / dialogs /
164â†’        // data state from prior app usage showing after reviewer exits if going to reviewer directly
165â†’        TaskStackBuilder
166â†’            .create(applicationContext)
167â†’            .addNextIntent(reloadIntent)
168â†’            .addNextIntent(reviewIntent)
169â†’            .startActivities()
170â†’        finish()
171â†’    }
172â†’
173â†’    private fun handleSyncIntent(
174â†’        reloadIntent: Intent,
175â†’        action: String?,
176â†’    ) {
177â†’        Timber.i("Handling Sync Intent")
178â†’        sendDoSyncMsg()
179â†’        reloadIntent.action = action
180â†’        reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
181â†’        startActivity(reloadIntent)
182â†’        finish()
183â†’    }
184â†’
185â†’    private fun handleFileImport(
186â†’        intent: Intent,
187â†’        reloadIntent: Intent,
188â†’        action: String?,
189â†’    ) {
190â†’        Timber.i("Handling file import")
191â†’        if (!hasShownAppIntro()) {
192â†’            Timber.i("Trying to import a file when the app was not started at all")
193â†’            showThemedToast(this, R.string.app_not_initialized_new, false)
194â†’            return
195â†’        }
196â†’        val importResult = handleFileImport(this, intent)
197â†’        // attempt to delete the downloaded deck if it is a shared deck download import
198â†’        if (intent.hasExtra(SharedDecksDownloadFragment.EXTRA_IS_SHARED_DOWNLOAD)) {
199â†’            deleteDownloadedDeck(intent.data)
200â†’        }
201â†’
202â†’        // Start DeckPicker if we correctly processed ACTION_VIEW
203â†’        if (importResult.isSuccess) {
204â†’            deleteImportedDeck(intent.data?.path)
205â†’            reloadIntent.action = action
206â†’            reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
207â†’            startActivity(reloadIntent)
208â†’            finish()
209â†’        } else {
210â†’            Timber.i("File import failed")
211â†’            // Don't import the file if it didn't load properly or doesn't have apkg extension
212â†’            showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(this, importResult.humanReadableMessage, true)
213â†’        }
214â†’    }
215â†’
216â†’    private fun deleteImportedDeck(path: String?) {
217â†’        try {
218â†’            val file = File(path!!)
219â†’            val fileUri =
220â†’                applicationContext?.let {
221â†’                    FileProvider.getUriForFile(
222â†’                        it,
223â†’                        it.applicationContext?.packageName + ".apkgfileprovider",
224â†’                        File(it.getExternalFilesDir(FileUtil.getDownloadDirectory()), file.name),
225â†’                    )
226â†’                }
227â†’            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
228â†’            contentResolver.delete(fileUri!!, null, null)
229â†’            Timber.i("onCreate() import successful and downloaded file deleted")
230â†’        } catch (e: Exception) {
231â†’            Timber.w(e, "onCreate() import successful and cannot delete file")
232â†’        }
233â†’    }
234â†’
235â†’    private fun handleImageImport(data: Intent) {
236â†’        val imageUri =
237â†’            if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
238â†’                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Uri::class.java)
239â†’            } else {
240â†’                data.data
241â†’            }
242â†’
243â†’        val imageOcclusionIntentBuilder = ImageOcclusionIntentBuilder(this)
244â†’        val intentImageOcclusion = imageOcclusionIntentBuilder.buildIntent(imageUri)
245â†’
246â†’        TaskStackBuilder
247â†’            .create(this)
248â†’            .addNextIntentWithParentStack(Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java))
249â†’            .addNextIntent(intentImageOcclusion)
250â†’            .startActivities()
251â†’    }
252â†’
253â†’    private fun deleteDownloadedDeck(sharedDeckUri: Uri?) {
254â†’        if (sharedDeckUri == null) {
255â†’            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded a shared deck but uri was null when trying to delete its file")
256â†’            return
257â†’        }
258â†’
259â†’        try {
260â†’            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
261â†’            contentResolver.delete(sharedDeckUri, null, null)
262â†’            Timber.i("onCreate: downloaded shared deck deleted")
263â†’        } catch (e: Exception) {
264â†’            Timber.w(e, "onCreate: failed to delete downloaded shared deck")
265â†’        }
266â†’    }
267â†’
268â†’    private fun launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent: Intent) {
269â†’        // Launcher intents should start DeckPicker if no other task exists,
270â†’        // otherwise go to previous task
271â†’        Timber.i("Launching DeckPicker")
272â†’        reloadIntent.action = Intent.ACTION_MAIN
273â†’        reloadIntent.addCategory(Intent.CATEGORY_LAUNCHER)
274â†’        reloadIntent.flags = Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_NEW_TASK or Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TASK
275â†’        startActivityIfNeeded(reloadIntent, 0)
276â†’        finish()

Looking at the code, I can now see the issue. Let me trace through what happens when a CSV file is selected:

1. In `IntentHandler.kt`, `getLaunchType()` determines if the import is `TEXT_IMPORT`:
```kotlin
mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
    mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
```

2. Notice that the condition checks for `text/comma-separated-values` - but the standard MIME type for CSV is `text/csv`, not `text/comma-separated-values`.

```kotlin
extraMimes =
    arrayOf(
        TEXT_PLAIN,
        "text/comma-separated-values",
        "text/csv",
        "text/tab-separated-values",
    ),
```

Here we see that `text/csv` is being used as an extra MIME type when picking files, but in the `IntentHandler.getLaunchType()` it's not being recognized!

The issue is in `IntentHandler.kt`'s `getLaunchType` function:
- It checks for `text/tab-separated-values` and `text/comma-separated-values`
- But it doesn't check for `text/csv` which is a common MIME type for CSV files

So the fix needs to:
1. Add `text/csv` to the MIME type matching in `IntentHandler.getLaunchType()`
2. Add `text/csv` to the AndroidManifest.xml intent filter

Before my fix, AnkiDroid only recognized `text/comma-separated-values` (which is a less common MIME type) and `text/tab-separated-values` (for TSV files).

2. When a CSV file is shared to or opened with AnkiDroid, the intent will now be correctly categorized as `TEXT_IMPORT` instead of being incorrectly handled as `FILE_IMPORT`

This is a minimal, targeted fix that addresses the root cause of the issue without breaking existing functionality.

content.text:
## Summary

I have successfully fixed the issue where users couldn't see or open CSV files in AnkiDroid. 

### Root Cause

The problem was that `text/csv` - the most common MIME type for CSV files on Android - was not being recognized by AnkiDroid. The code only recognized:
- `text/tab-separated-values` (for TSV files)
- `text/comma-separated-values` (a less common CSV MIME type)

When a user opens a CSV file from their file manager, Android typically reports the MIME type as `text/csv`. Since AnkiDroid didn't recognize this MIME type, the CSV files wouldn't appear as openable with AnkiDroid, and if somehow received, they would be incorrectly processed.

### Changes Made

**1. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/IntentHandler.kt`** (line 338)
- Added `mimeType == "text/csv"` to the `getLaunchType()` function's condition for `LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT`
- This ensures CSV files with `text/csv` MIME type are correctly routed to the CSV import handler

**2. `AnkiDroid/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`** (line 241)
- Added `<data android:mimeType="text/csv"/>` to the MIME type matcher intent-filter
- This allows AnkiDroid to appear in the share/open-with menu when a user tries to open a CSV file from their file manager

### Why This Fix Works

The fix ensures that CSV files with any of the three common MIME types are now properly handled:
- `text/csv` (most common, now added)
- `text/comma-separated-values` (already supported)
- `text/tab-separated-values` (already supported, for TSV files)

This is consistent with the file picker in `ImportFileSelectionFragment.kt` which already had `text/csv` in its `extraMimes` array for the file selection dialog - the issue was just that incoming intents with this MIME type weren't being recognized.

 53â†’/**
 54â†’ * Class which handles how the application responds to different intents, forcing it to always be single task,
 55â†’ * but allowing custom behavior depending on the intent
 56â†’ * It inherits from [AbstractIntentHandler]
 57â†’ *
 58â†’ * @author Tim
 59â†’ */
 60â†’class IntentHandler : AbstractIntentHandler() {
 61â†’    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
 62â†’        // Note: This is our entry point from the launcher with intent: android.intent.action.MAIN
 63â†’        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
 64â†’        val intent = intent
 65â†’        Timber.v(intent.toString())
 66â†’        val reloadIntent = Intent(this, DeckPicker::class.java)
 67â†’        reloadIntent.setDataAndType(getIntent().data, getIntent().type)
 68â†’        val action = intent.action
 69â†’        // #6157 - We want to block actions that need permissions we don't have, but not the default case
 70â†’        // as this requires nothing
 71â†’        val runIfStoragePermissions = { runnable: () -> Unit -> performActionIfStorageAccessible(reloadIntent, action) { runnable() } }
 72â†’        val launchType = getLaunchType(intent)
 73â†’        // TODO block the UI with some kind of ProgressDialog instead of cancelling the sync work
 74â†’        if (requiresCollectionAccess(launchType)) {
 75â†’            SyncWorker.cancel(this)
 76â†’        }
 77â†’        when (launchType) {
 78â†’            LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT ->
 79â†’                runIfStoragePermissions {
 80â†’                    handleFileImport(fileIntent, reloadIntent, action)
 81â†’                    finish()
 82â†’                }
 83â†’            LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT ->
 84â†’                runIfStoragePermissions {
 85â†’                    onSelectedCsvForImport(fileIntent)
 86â†’                    finish()
 87â†’                }
 88â†’            LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT ->
 89â†’                runIfStoragePermissions {
 90â†’                    handleImageImport(intent)
 91â†’                    finish()
 92â†’                }
 93â†’            LaunchType.SYNC -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleSyncIntent(reloadIntent, action) }
 94â†’            LaunchType.REVIEW -> runIfStoragePermissions { handleReviewIntent(reloadIntent, intent) }
 95â†’            LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW -> {
 96â†’                Timber.d("onCreate() performing default action")
 97â†’                launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
 98â†’            }
 99â†’            LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO -> {
100â†’                copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent)
101â†’                finish()
102â†’            }
103â†’        }
104â†’    }
105â†’
106â†’    private fun copyDebugInfoToClipboard(intent: Intent) {
107â†’        Timber.i("Copying debug info to clipboard")
108â†’        // null string is handled by copyToClipboard in try-catch
109â†’        this.copyToClipboard(
110â†’            text = (intent.getStringExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA)!!),
111â†’            failureMessageId = R.string.about_ankidroid_error_copy_debug_info,
112â†’        )
113â†’    }

115â†’    private val fileIntent: Intent
116â†’        get() {
117â†’            return if (intent.action == Intent.ACTION_SEND) {
118â†’                IntentCompat.getParcelableExtra(intent, Intent.EXTRA_STREAM, Intent::class.java) ?: intent
119â†’            } else {
120â†’                intent
121â†’            }
122â†’        }

124â†’    /**
125â†’     * Execute the runnable if one of the two following conditions are satisfied:
126â†’     *
127â†’     *  * AnkiDroid is using an app-private directory to store user data
128â†’     *  * AnkiDroid is using a legacy directory to store user data but has access to it since storage permission
129â†’     * has been granted (as long as AnkiDroid targeted API < 30, requested legacy storage, and has not been uninstalled since)
130â†’     *
131â†’     */
132â†’    @NeedsTest("clicking a file in 'Files' to import")
133â†’    private fun performActionIfStorageAccessible(
134â†’        reloadIntent: Intent,
135â†’        action: String?,
136â†’        block: () -> Unit,
137â†’    ) {
138â†’        if (grantedStoragePermissions(this, showToast = true)) {
139â†’            Timber.i("User has storage permissions. Running intent: %s", action)
140â†’            block()
141â†’        } else {
142â†’            Timber.i("No Storage Permission, cancelling intent '%s'", action)
143â†’            launchDeckPickerIfNoOtherTasks(reloadIntent)
144â†’        }
145â†’    }

147â†’    private fun handleReviewIntent(
148â†’        reloadIntent: Intent,
149â†’        reviewerIntent: Intent,
150â†’    ) {
151â†’        val deckId = intent.getLongExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, 0)
152â†’        Timber.i("Handling intent to review deck '%d'", deckId)
153â†’
154â†’        val reviewIntent =
155â†’            if (sharedPrefs().getBoolean("newReviewer", false)) {
156â†’                ReviewerFragment.getIntent(this)
157â†’            } else {
158â†’                Intent(this, Reviewer::class.java).apply {
159â†’                    addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
160â†’                }
161â†’            }
162â†’        CollectionManager.getColUnsafe().decks.select(deckId)
163â†’        // Clean the stack out under the reviewer to avoid any incorrect activities / dialogs /
164â†’        // data state from prior app usage showing after reviewer exits if going to reviewer directly
165â†’        TaskStackBuilder
166â†’            .create(applicationContext)
167â†’            .addNextIntent(reloadIntent)
168â†’            .addNextIntent(reviewIntent)
169â†’            .startActivities()
170â†’        finish()
171â†’    }

174â†’        reloadIntent: Intent,

176â†’    ) {
177â†’        Timber.i("Handling Sync Intent")
178â†’        sendDoSyncMsg()
179â†’        reloadIntent.action = action
180â†’        reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
181â†’        startActivity(reloadIntent)
182â†’        finish()

185â†’    private fun handleFileImport(
186â†’        intent: Intent,
187â†’        reloadIntent: Intent,
188â†’        action: String?,
189â†’    ) {
190â†’        Timber.i("Handling file import")
191â†’        if (!hasShownAppIntro()) {
192â†’            Timber.i("Trying to import a file when the app was not started at all")
193â†’            showThemedToast(this, R.string.app_not_initialized_new, false)
194â†’            return
195â†’        }
196â†’        val importResult = handleFileImport(this, intent)
197â†’        // attempt to delete the downloaded deck if it is a shared deck download import
198â†’        if (intent.hasExtra(SharedDecksDownloadFragment.EXTRA_IS_SHARED_DOWNLOAD)) {
199â†’            deleteDownloadedDeck(intent.data)
200â†’        }
201â†’
202â†’        // Start DeckPicker if we correctly processed ACTION_VIEW
203â†’        if (importResult.isSuccess) {
204â†’            deleteImportedDeck(intent.data?.path)
205â†’            reloadIntent.action = action
206â†’            reloadIntent.addFlags(Intent.FLAG_ACTIVITY_CLEAR_TOP)
207â†’            startActivity(reloadIntent)
208â†’            finish()
209â†’        } else {
210â†’            Timber.i("File import failed")
211â†’            // Don't import the file if it didn't load properly or doesn't have apkg extension
212â†’            showImportUnsuccessfulDialog(this, importResult.humanReadableMessage, true)
213â†’        }
214â†’    }

216â†’    private fun deleteImportedDeck(path: String?) {
217â†’        try {
218â†’            val file = File(path!!)
219â†’            val fileUri =
220â†’                applicationContext?.let {
221â†’                    FileProvider.getUriForFile(
222â†’                        it,
223â†’                        it.applicationContext?.packageName + ".apkgfileprovider",
224â†’                        File(it.getExternalFilesDir(FileUtil.getDownloadDirectory()), file.name),
225â†’                    )
226â†’                }
227â†’            // TODO move the file deletion on a background thread
228â†’            contentResolver.delete(fileUri!!, null, null)
229â†’            Timber.i("onCreate() import successful and downloaded file deleted")
230â†’        } catch (e: Exception) {
231â†’            Timber.w(e, "onCreate() import successful and cannot delete file")
232â†’        }
233â†’    }

302â†’        private fun isValidViewIntent(intent: Intent): Boolean {
303â†’            // Negating a negative because we want to call specific attention to the fact that it's invalid
304â†’            // #6312 - Smart Launcher provided an empty ACTION_VIEW, no point in importing here.
305â†’            return !isInvalidViewIntent(intent)
306â†’        }

308â†’        /** Checks whether storage permissions are granted on the device. If the device is not using legacy storage,
309â†’         *  it verifies if the app has been granted the necessary storage access permission.
310â†’         *  @return `true`: if granted, otherwise `false` and shows a missing permission toast
311â†’         */
312â†’        fun grantedStoragePermissions(
313â†’            context: Context,
314â†’            showToast: Boolean,
315â†’        ): Boolean {
316â†’            val granted =
317â†’                !ScopedStorageService.isLegacyStorage(context) ||
318â†’                    hasLegacyStorageAccessPermission(context) ||
319â†’                    Permissions.isExternalStorageManagerCompat()
320â†’
321â†’            if (!granted && showToast) {
322â†’                showThemedToast(context, context.getString(R.string.intent_handler_failed_no_storage_permission), false)
323â†’            }
324â†’
325â†’            return granted
326â†’        }

328â†’        @VisibleForTesting
329â†’        @CheckResult
330â†’        fun getLaunchType(intent: Intent): LaunchType {
331â†’            val action = intent.action
332â†’            return if (action == Intent.ACTION_SEND || Intent.ACTION_VIEW == action && isValidViewIntent(intent)) {
333â†’                val mimeType = intent.resolveMimeType()
334â†’                when {
335â†’                    mimeType?.startsWith("image/") == true -> LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT
336â†’                    mimeType == "text/tab-separated-values" ||
337â†’                        mimeType == "text/comma-separated-values" -> LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT
338â†’                    else -> LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT
339â†’                }
340â†’            } else if ("com.ichi2.anki.DO_SYNC" == action) {
341â†’                LaunchType.SYNC
342â†’            } else if (intent.hasExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID)) {
343â†’                LaunchType.REVIEW
344â†’            } else if (action == CLIPBOARD_INTENT) {
345â†’                LaunchType.COPY_DEBUG_INFO
346â†’            } else {
347â†’                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW
348â†’            }
349â†’        }

359â†’        fun copyStringToClipboardIntent(
360â†’            context: Context,
361â†’            textToCopy: String,
362â†’        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).also {
363â†’            it.action = CLIPBOARD_INTENT

366â†’            it.putExtra(CLIPBOARD_INTENT_EXTRA_DATA, textToCopy.trimToLength(25000))

369â†’        fun requiresCollectionAccess(launchType: LaunchType): Boolean =
370â†’            when (launchType) {
371â†’                LaunchType.SYNC,
372â†’                LaunchType.REVIEW,
373â†’                LaunchType.DEFAULT_START_APP_IF_NEW,
374â†’                LaunchType.FILE_IMPORT,
375â†’                LaunchType.TEXT_IMPORT,
376â†’                LaunchType.IMAGE_IMPORT,

381â†’        class DoSync :
382â†’            DialogHandlerMessage(
383â†’                which = WhichDialogHandler.MSG_DO_SYNC,
384â†’                analyticName = "DoSyncDialog",
385â†’            ) {
386â†’            override fun handleAsyncMessage(activity: AnkiActivity) {
387â†’                // we may be called via any AnkiActivity but sync is a DeckPicker thing
388â†’                if (activity !is DeckPicker) {
389â†’                    showError(
390â†’                        activity,
391â†’                        activity.getString(R.string.something_wrong),
392â†’                        ClassCastException(activity.javaClass.simpleName + " is not " + DeckPicker.javaClass.simpleName),
393â†’                        true,
394â†’                    )
395â†’                    return
396â†’                }
397â†’                // let's be clear about the type now that we've checked
398â†’                val deckPicker = activity
399â†’
400â†’                val preferences = deckPicker.sharedPrefs()
401â†’                val res = deckPicker.resources
402â†’                val hkey = preferences.getString("hkey", "")
403â†’                val millisecondsSinceLastSync = millisecondsSinceLastSync(preferences)
404â†’                val limited = millisecondsSinceLastSync < INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL
405â†’                if (!limited && hkey!!.isNotEmpty() && NetworkUtils.isOnline) {
406â†’                    deckPicker.sync()
407â†’                } else {
408â†’                    val err = res.getString(R.string.sync_error)
409â†’                    if (limited) {
410â†’                        val remainingTimeInSeconds =
411â†’                            max((INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL - millisecondsSinceLastSync) / 1000, 1)
412â†’                        // getQuantityString needs an int
413â†’                        val remaining = min(Int.MAX_VALUE.toLong(), remainingTimeInSeconds).toInt()
414â†’                        val message =
415â†’                            res.getQuantityString(
416â†’                                R.plurals.sync_automatic_sync_needs_more_time,
417â†’                                remaining,
418â†’                                remaining,
419â†’                            )
420â†’                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(err, message, Channel.SYNC)
421â†’                    } else {
422â†’                        deckPicker.showSimpleNotification(
423â†’                            err,
424â†’                            res.getString(R.string.youre_offline),
425â†’                            Channel.SYNC,
426â†’                        )
427â†’                    }
428â†’                }
429â†’                deckPicker.finish()
430â†’            }
431â†’
432â†’            override fun toMessage(): Message = emptyMessage(this.what)
433â†’
434â†’            companion object {
435â†’                const val INTENT_SYNC_MIN_INTERVAL =
436â†’                    (
437â†’                        2 * 60000 // 2min minimum sync interval
438â†’                    ).toLong()
439â†’            }
440â†’        }
441â†’
442â†’        /**
443â†’         * Returns an intent to review a specific deck.
444â†’         * This does not states which reviewer to use, instead IntentHandler will choose whether to use the
445â†’         * legacy or the new reviewer based on the "newReviewer" preference.
446â†’         * It is expected to be used from widget, shortcut, reminders but not from ankidroid directly because of the CLEAR_TOP flag.
447â†’         */
448â†’        fun intentToReviewDeckFromShorcuts(
449â†’            context: Context,
450â†’            deckId: DeckId,
451â†’        ) = Intent(context, IntentHandler::class.java).apply {
452â†’            setAction(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
453â†’            putExtra(ReminderService.EXTRA_DECK_ID, deckId)
454â†’        }
455â†’    }
456â†’}
